nameMeanings.name: Benjamin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Jewish (Sephardic and Ashkenazic), English, French, West Indian (mainly Haiti), and African (mainly Nigeria and Tanzania); Hungarian (Benjámin): from the Hebrew male personal name Binyāmīn ‘Son of the South’. In the Book of Genesis, it is treated as meaning ‘Son of the Right Hand’. The two senses are connected, since in Hebrew the south is thought of as the right-hand side of a person who is facing east. Benjamin was the youngest and favorite son of Jacob and supposed progenitor of one of the twelve tribes of Israel (Genesis 35:16-18; 42:4). The personal name was not common among Gentiles in the Middle Ages, but its use was sanctioned by virtue of having been borne by a Christian saint martyred in Persia in about AD 424. In some cases in medieval Europe it was also applied as a byname or nickname to the youngest (and beloved) son of a large family; this is the sense of modern French benjamin. In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Assyrian/Chaldean Benyamin and Italian Beniamino.
History: John Benjamin (1598–1645) came from England to Watertown, MA, in 1632. Jean-Baptiste Benjamin dit Saint-Aubin from France married Jeanne Allard in QC in 1704.

variants.name: Benyamin
meanings: Jewish, Assyrian/Chaldean, and Arabic (Egypt): from the Hebrew and Syriac personal name Benyāmīn, a variant of Binyāmīn (see Benjamin ).

variants.name: Benowitz
meanings: Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): patronymic from a short form of the personal name Beniamin (see Benjamin ). The ending -owitz is a Germanized form of the East Slavic patronymic ending -ovich, Polish -owicz.

variants.name: Ben
meanings: Native American (mainly Navajo) and apparently also African American: adoption of the English personal name Ben (short form of Benjamin ) as a surname.
Italian (also Del Ben): from a dialect form of the adverb bene ‘well’. This form, without the preposition, is found only in Belluno province, in particular in Taibon Agordino.

Indian (Gujarat): from Gujarati ben ‘sister’, from Sanskrit bhaginī, a title often attached to their personal name by Gujarati women. It is not a true surname, but is sometimes used as a surname by women who do not have a surname.

variants.name: Benzon
meanings: Danish: patronymic from a short form of the personal name   Benjamin   or   Bernhard  , of German origin (compare   Benson  ).

Filipino: Chinese Filipino surname formed with Chinese 孫 or sūn ‘grandchild’ as the second element. The first element is usually a reference to birth order or a prominent characteristic of the person.

nameMeanings.name: Mabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a nickname from the Old French vocabulary word amabel, amable ‘lovely’ (akin to modern English amiable ‘friendly, good-humoured’). The initial vowel began to be lost as early as the 12th century (the same woman is referred to as both Mabilia and Amabilia in a document of 1185 ), but a short vowel in the resulting first syllable was standard, giving a rhyme with babble, until the 19th century, when people began to pronounce the name to rhyme with table.

en.lifeSummary: Mabel Benjamin was born about 1430, in Ipswich, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. She married John Cutler I in 1445, in Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died about 1480, in her hometown, at the age of 52, and was buried in Ipswich, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom.

Elle est décédée environ 1480, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 52 ans, et a été enterrée à Ipswich, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Mabel Benjaminは約1430年にイギリスイギリススフォルクイプスウィチで生まれた。1445年にイギリススフォルクでJohn Cutler Iと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼女は約1480年に故郷で52歳で死去し，イギリスイギリススフォルクイプスウィチにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Mabel Benjamin(은)는 대략 1430년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 이프스위치에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1445년 영국 수폴크에서 John Cutler I(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1480년 그녀의 고향에서 52세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 이프스위치에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Mabel Benjamin родилась около 1430 г. в местности ипсвич, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Cutler I в 1445 г. в местности суффолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла около 1480 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 52 лет и была похоронена в местности ипсвич, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания.
